Public demo 022 · Claim boundary · Evidence Docket 6.1

A claim enters.
The firewall
decides.

Evidence decides. Run a browser-local proof firewall where a public claim must survive provenance, contradiction, risk, replay, public/private boundary, and human-review gates before it becomes a publishable Evidence Docket.

Open the firewall Selection Gate

No user data wanted. Use demo text only. Do not enter personal, confidential, regulated, customer, wallet, seed phrase, private-key, or proprietary information. This page uses no account, no cookies, no analytics, no storage, no wallet, no network request, and no external action.

Proof roomAWAITING CLAIM

Claim firewall

Choose a public-safe claim.

The firewall is deterministic and local. It does not verify real-world truth. It teaches the publication discipline: no Evidence Docket, no strong public claim.

Verifier Mesh

Awaiting docket review.

Run the firewall to see whether the claim can be published, quarantined, revised, or rejected.

PENDING
Proof integrity—
Publication readiness—
Claim boundary—
Recommended action—

Claims matrix

    Verifier findings

      Risk and replay

        Public / private boundary

        Publish proof, not private intelligence.

        Public dockets should show commitments, hashes, summaries, gates, decisions, and claim boundaries. Private prompts, raw customer data, privileged workpapers, and sensitive traces stay outside the public surface.

        Falsification posture

        A strong system can show failure.

        If evidence is missing, contradictions are unresolved, replay is absent, or risk is too high, the claim is not published as strong. It returns to evidence gathering or human review.

        Downloadable docket

        Every run emits a public-safe artifact.

        The generated JSON makes the demo inspectable: claim, gates, evidence posture, risk state, replay path, verdict, and non-claims are recorded for review.

        Chronicle trace

        What happened?

          Docket JSON

          Public-safe Evidence Docket

          Run the Verifier Mesh to generate a docket.
          GoalOS publication law

          No proof, no settlement. No eval, no propagation. No rollback, no release.

          The firewall shows the social contract of GoalOS: claims do not advance because they sound impressive. They advance only when evidence survives review and remains bounded by what was actually shown.

          ClaimEvidenceContradictionRiskReplayBoundaryHuman reviewChronicle
